topic: A person finds their spouse's secret savings account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The envelope felt thick, heavy with unspoken accusations. I had been searching for misplaced receipts, something trivial, and instead, my fingers brushed against a bank statement tucked deep inside an old book. My breath hitched. It was a savings account, a substantial one, entirely unknown to me. A slow, hot flush crept up my neck, turning my skin tight. I stared at the figures, the numbers mocking the comfortable illusion I’d built with my partner. A tremor started in my hands, and I crumpled the paper, the sound sharp in the quiet apartment. Every surface seemed too bright, too loud. I needed air, needed distance from this suffocating realization.

[story 2]

It was the sheer audacity of it that unsettled me. I found the ledger tucked beneath the loose floorboard in the study—a neatly bound book detailing transactions I hadn't seen. My jaw tightened so hard I thought my teeth might crack. The silence of the house amplified the frantic drumming in my chest. I flipped through the pages, the ink blurring as I scanned the deposits, the hidden accumulations. A low, guttural sound escaped my throat, something raw and uncontrolled. I slammed the book shut, the sound echoing like a gunshot. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of the room, my movements jerky and sharp, unable to settle my gaze on anything.

[story 3]

The discovery hit me like a physical blow. I was sitting at the kitchen table, ostensibly organizing mail, when I noticed the misplaced folder. Inside, the details were laid bare: a secret account, funded over months, entirely outside the purview of our shared finances. My vision tunneled. I gripped the edge of the table until my knuckles were white, the pressure building until my vision swam. I pushed back from the table, the chair scraping loudly against the tile floor. I began to walk, a rigid, relentless stride toward the bedroom, the energy vibrating off me, demanding release.
